title: Feature: Worktree Enforcement in Agent Hooks — close the gap between ADR-004 and actual agent behavior
status_note: Critical — without this, all swarm features bypass worktree isolation

steps.description: Extend SessionState in hook.rs with worktree_path (Option<String>) and allowed_paths (Vec<String>). These fields track the active worktree and permitted file paths for the current task. Update save/load to handle new fields with serde defaults for backward compatibility.
steps.done_condition: SessionState serializes/deserializes with new fields; existing session files load without error (serde defaults)

steps.description: Add workplan step resolution to pre-agent hook. When HEXFLO_TASK:{uuid} is present: (1) load active workplan JSON from docs/workplans/, (2) match task title against workplan steps (fuzzy match on step description or step-N ID), (3) extract worktree_branch and layer/adapter fields. Store resolved worktree_branch in session state. If workplan not found or step not matched, warn but allow (fail-open).
steps.done_condition: pre-agent resolves task UUID to workplan step; worktree_branch stored in session state; fail-open when workplan missing

steps.description: Add worktree auto-creation in pre-agent hook. After resolving worktree_branch: (1) run git worktree list to check if it exists, (2) if not, run git worktree add <path> -b <branch> from project root, (3) path format: ../{project}-worktrees/feat/{feature}/{adapter}, (4) validate branch name matches feat/*/* pattern, (5) store worktree_path in session state, (6) output HEXFLO_WORKTREE_PATH=<path> to stdout so the orchestrator can include it in the agent prompt. Respect MAX_WORKTREES=8 limit — block if at capacity.
steps.done_condition: Worktree auto-created on first agent spawn for a task; path output to stdout; blocked at 8 concurrent worktrees; branch naming validated

steps.description: Add adapter boundary enforcement to pre-edit hook. When session state has allowed_paths (derived from task's layer/adapter): (1) resolve the file path being edited from TOOL_INPUT, (2) check if it falls within the allowed boundary, (3) docs/, tests/, config/ always allowed (cross-cutting), (4) if outside boundary and mode is mandatory: block with exit 2 and message showing allowed paths, (5) if advisory: warn but allow. Boundary mapping: domain→domain/, ports→ports/, adapters/secondary/{name}→files matching adapter name, usecases→usecases/+composition-root, integration→tests/.
steps.done_condition: pre-edit blocks edits outside adapter boundary in mandatory mode; warns in advisory mode; docs/tests always allowed

steps.description: Add dependency order enforcement to pre-agent hook. Before allowing a task to start: (1) load workplan steps, (2) find current step's dependencies[], (3) for each dependency, resolve its HexFlo task ID and check status via GET /api/swarms/tasks/{id}, (4) if any dependency is not 'completed': block with exit 2 and message listing incomplete dependencies, (5) cache dependency check result in session state for 30s to avoid repeated API calls. Fail-open if nexus is unreachable.
steps.done_condition: pre-agent blocks task when dependencies incomplete; allows when all deps completed; fail-open when nexus unreachable

steps.description: Add auto-merge and cleanup to subagent-stop hook. After marking a task complete: (1) check if all tasks in the same tier are completed, (2) if yes, run feature-workflow.sh merge for that tier's worktrees (in dependency order), (3) if merge succeeds, run feature-workflow.sh cleanup for merged worktrees, (4) if ALL workplan tasks are complete, update workplan status to 'done'. Log all merge/cleanup operations. Do not auto-merge if any task in the tier failed.
steps.done_condition: Tier completion triggers automatic merge in dependency order; cleanup removes merged worktrees; workplan marked done when all complete

steps.description: Add hex agent audit command to hex-cli. Scans git log for commits by agents (Co-Authored-By: Claude) and cross-references with HexFlo task completions. Flags: (1) commits on main without associated worktree branch, (2) agents that edited files outside their assigned boundary, (3) tasks completed without matching git commits. Output as a compliance report with pass/warn/fail per task.
steps.done_condition: hex agent audit produces compliance report; flags main-branch agent edits and boundary violations

steps.description: Integration tests: (1) spawn mock background agent with HEXFLO_TASK — verify worktree auto-created, (2) attempt edit outside boundary — verify blocked in mandatory mode, (3) spawn task with incomplete dependency — verify blocked, (4) complete all tier tasks — verify auto-merge triggered, (5) hex agent audit on test commits — verify report flags violations. Run hex analyze to verify no boundary violations in the enforcement code itself.
steps.done_condition: All integration tests pass; hex analyze clean; enforcement hooks work end-to-end

mergeOrder: step-1 (session state — tier 0, all others depend on it)
step-1 → step-2, step-4 parallel (workplan resolution + boundary check — tier 1)
step-2 → step-3, step-5 parallel (auto-create + dependency order — tier 2)
step-3 + step-5 → step-6 (auto-merge — needs worktrees + dependency checking)
step-3 + step-4 → step-7 (audit — needs worktree tracking + boundary rules)
step-6 + step-7 → step-8 (integration tests — all features)

riskRegister.risk: Worktree creation fails due to uncommitted changes on main
riskRegister.mitigation: pre-agent checks git status before worktree add; stash uncommitted changes or block with message

riskRegister.risk: Workplan JSON format inconsistency — step IDs don't match HexFlo task titles
riskRegister.mitigation: Fuzzy matching on step description; include step-N ID in HexFlo task title as convention
Rebase-first strategy (ADR-004); if conflict detected, block auto-merge and notify user via inbox (ADR-060)

riskRegister.risk: Disk exhaustion from too many worktrees
riskRegister.mitigation: MAX_WORKTREES=8 enforced; stale detection flags worktrees >24h without commits

riskRegister.risk: Hook latency — worktree resolution adds time to every agent spawn
riskRegister.mitigation: Cache workplan resolution in session state; git worktree list is fast (<50ms)

successCriteria: Background agent with HEXFLO_TASK automatically gets its own worktree (not main)
Agent attempting to edit file outside its adapter boundary is blocked with clear message
Agent cannot start until its dependency tasks are completed
Completing all tier tasks triggers automatic merge in correct dependency order
hex agent audit correctly flags main-branch agent edits as violations
Entire enforcement pipeline adds <3s to agent spawn time
Existing non-worktree workflows (foreground agents, read-only agents) are unaffected
